# UC-14.6.7 · Modbus Device Identification Enumeration (FC 43 / 0x2B)

## Description

Read Device Identification (FC 0x2B) is a common reconnaissance step; bursts from non-inventory hosts often precede targeted attacks or rogue integration attempts.

## Value

Read Device Identification (FC 0x2B) is a common reconnaissance step; bursts from non-inventory hosts often precede targeted attacks or rogue integration attempts.

## Implementation

Forward ICSNPP Modbus detailed and read-device-identification logs from OT VLAN taps. Allowlist asset-management scanners; alert on new sources or high fan-out to many slaves in a short window.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` or `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_read_device_identification:json"` (`func`, `mei_type`, `object_id`, `source_h`, `destination_h`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward ICSNPP Modbus detailed and read-device-identification logs from OT VLAN taps. Allowlist asset-management scanners; alert on new sources or high fan-out to many slaves in a short window.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot (sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json" OR sourcetype="zeek:modbus_read_device_identification:json")
| where func IN ("0x2B","43","0x2b") OR mei_type="READ-DEVICE-IDENTIFICATION" OR isnotnull(object_id)
| stats count dc(destination_h) as targets by source_h
| where count>20 OR targets>5
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Modbus Device Identification Enumeration (FC 43 / 0x2B)** — Read Device Identification (FC 0x2B) is a common reconnaissance step; bursts from non-inventory hosts often precede targeted attacks or rogue integration attempts.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` or `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_read_device_identification:json"` (`func`, `mei_type`, `object_id`, `source_h`, `destination_h`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:modbus_detailed:json, zeek:modbus_read_device_identification:json. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where func IN ("0x2B","43","0x2b") OR mei_type="READ-DEVICE-IDENTIFICATION" OR isnotnull(object_id)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by source_h** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>20 OR targets>5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a short window and compare key fields with Zeek or Corelight protocol logs on the sensor for the same flow. Reconcile function codes, endpoints, and time order against a small known-good capture if needed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (enumeration events by source), Table (source, target count), Map or table (distinct targets).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot (sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json" OR sourcetype="zeek:modbus_read_device_identification:json")
| where func IN ("0x2B","43","0x2b") OR mei_type="READ-DEVICE-IDENTIFICATION" OR isnotnull(object_id)
| stats count dc(destination_h) as targets by source_h
| where count>20 OR targets>5
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (enumeration events by source), Table (source, target count), Map or table (distinct targets).
